highlights.summary: This change set makes localized thread-safety and reliability edits in APSManager.swift: it removes an unused viewContext (diff:be1e0dbd060a, commit:e274ed1d9c3d), moves tir() out of an inner privateContext.perform closure to a plain synchronous helper (diff:be1e0dbd060a), adds [weak self] to multiple Combine .sink closures and to privateContext.perform closures to avoid strong self captures (diff:be1e0dbd060a, commit:e274ed1d9c3d), changes several Combine publishers that previously .receive(on: processQueue) to .receive(on: DispatchQueue.main) for properties that are also read from the main thread (scheduledBasal, suspended, manualTempBasal) (diff:be1e0dbd060a, commit:e274ed1d9c3d), replaces force-unwrapped Date usages by capturing Date() into a local endDate variable before assigning and computing durations (diff:be1e0dbd060a), and adds early-guard / weak-self checks inside privateContext.perform blocks (diff:be1e0dbd060a). The merge commit records this as "APSManager thread safety fixes (Part 1)" (commit:0bc038ccdbaa).
highlights.userFacingImpact: These are internal thread-safety and null-safety changes. They are intended to reduce race conditions between background processing and main-thread UI reads (by receiving certain Combine publishers on DispatchQueue.main) and to avoid crashes from strong self captures and force-unwrapped values. There is no explicit evidence in the supplied diff that dosing logic or algorithm outputs were intentionally changed; a human review is required to confirm there are no behavioral changes to loop/dosing logic.

items.userFacingImpact: These are internal thread-safety and null-safety changes. They are intended to reduce race conditions between background processing and main-thread UI reads (by receiving certain Combine publishers on DispatchQueue.main) and to avoid crashes from strong self captures and force-unwrapped values. There is no explicit evidence in the supplied diff that dosing logic or algorithm outputs were intentionally changed; a human review is required to confirm there are no behavioral changes to loop/dosing logic.

items.summary: Bumps the APP_DEV_VERSION build variable in Config.xcconfig from 0.8.3.2 to 0.8.3.3. The change is recorded in the commit titled "CI: Bump APP_DEV_VERSION to 0.8.3.3 [skip ci]" and shown in the diff for Config.xcconfig (diff:4fea9e53fd18, file:c0fa4a48f07f, commit:dbc10266177e).
items.userFacingImpact: No direct user-facing behavior changes are evident in the patch; it only updates an internal development version variable in a configuration file (Config.xcconfig) (file:c0fa4a48f07f, diff:4fea9e53fd18).

includedPullRequests.title: APSManager thread safety fixes (Part 1)
includedPullRequests.body:
This will be the first of at least 3 Pull requests targeted at optimizing the thread safety of the APSManager. As suggested by @kingst in PR #1145 I splitted my initial PR in several smaller PRs to be able to better review the changes made.

Changes made in this PR:

- remove unused `viewContext`
- move `tir()` out of a nested perform closure
- add `[weak self]` to combine sinks and `privateContext` perform closures
- move combine operations to receive on the main thread to avoid race conditions
- remove force unwraps
